mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales signals firm household demand and near-term revenue momentum for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is not associated with higher 20-day S&P 500 returns, with Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses upward through 0.50%, the market prices the start of a tightening impulse that raises discount rates and reduces equity duration values, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS upward crossings are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward instances.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a falling unemployment rate identifies a labor-market expansion that supports income, consumption, and earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-up unemployment-down state has lower mean 60-day S&P 500 returns than all other states over two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production weakness paired with rising consumer prices signals margin pressure from weak real output and sticky inflation, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO-down CPI-up episodes do not produce lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered episodes at p < 0.10 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts drop below 1200 thousand units signals weakening construction demand and a negative lead for cyclical earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below 1200 is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment of more than 0.3 percentage point over three months signals recession-risk repricing and weakening household income prospects, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-spike triggers have a nonnegative average 20-day S&P 500 return or a hit rate below 55% for negative returns in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in CPI growth raises expected real-rate tightening and compresses equity valuation multiples before earnings adjust, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 1-month change exceeds its prior 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if CPI acceleration states do not show lower 5-day S&P 500 returns when FEDFUNDS is rising than when FEDFUNDS is flat or falling.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth while the policy rate remains below 1% represents a low-discount-rate growth regime that lifts expected cash flows without materially raising discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the GDPC1-up FEDFUNDS-below-1% regime has lower average 60-day S&P 500 returns than all other regimes over two consecutive 4-year walk-forward windows.
